tables.description: Tracks employee attendance records including check-in and check-out times for each work date.
Stores baggage details linked to flights, including tag numbers and current status.
Records baggage loading events onto specific flights with timestamps.
Logs baggage scan events at various locations with timestamps for tracking.
Records baggage unloading events from flights with timestamps.
Tracks boarding preparation tasks for turnaround operations, including gate readiness and baggage loading status.

columns.description: Indicates if gate is ready for boarding
Indicates if baggage loading is complete

tables.description: Manages catering tasks for turnaround operations with vendor details and timing.
Tracks cleaning tasks for turnaround operations with assigned employees and timing.
Defines organizational departments with unique names.
Stores employee information including name and department assignment.
Maps employees to their assigned roles.
Tracks equipment details including name, status, and type classification.
Records equipment assignments to employees with assignment and return dates.
Defines equipment type categories.
Stores flight information including flight number and operational status.
Manages airport gate information and availability status.
Links gates to flights with assignment timestamps.
Tracks employee leave requests including type, date range, and approval status.
Records lost baggage incidents with descriptions and resolution status.

Current status of lost baggage case

tables.description: Logs equipment maintenance history with dates and remarks.
Tracks maintenance requests for equipment with descriptions and status.
Manages refueling tasks for turnaround operations with fuel quantities and status.
Defines role names for employee role assignments.
Links employees to shifts on specific dates for workforce scheduling.

Links stands to flights with assignment timestamps.
Tracks employee training completion with training names and dates.
Manages checklist items for turnaround operations with completion status.

tables.description: Manages turnaround operations for flights with timing targets and status.

entity_relationships.business_meaning: An attendance record belongs to one employee
A baggage item is associated with one flight
An employee belongs to one department and can be assigned multiple roles
A leave request is submitted by one employee
A schedule assigns one employee to one shift
A training record belongs to one employee
A turnaround operation is performed for one flight

business_processes.tables: attendance, department, employee, employee_role, leave_request, role, schedule, shift, training_record

use_cases.name: Flight Turnaround Management
use_cases.description: End-to-end management of aircraft turnaround operations including boarding, catering, cleaning, refueling, and checklist completion to ensure on-time departures.

use_cases.name: Baggage Tracking and Tracing
use_cases.description: Complete lifecycle tracking of baggage from check-in through loading, in-transit scans, unloading, and exception handling for lost baggage.

use_cases.name: Employee Attendance and Scheduling
use_cases.description: Workforce management covering shift scheduling, time-and-attendance tracking, leave requests, role assignments, and training compliance for airport staff.

use_cases.name: Equipment Maintenance Management
use_cases.description: Asset lifecycle management for ground support equipment including maintenance requests, service history, type categorization, and crew assignments.

use_cases.name: Gate and Stand Assignment
use_cases.description: Real-time allocation and monitoring of gate and stand resources for arriving and departing flights to optimize apron utilization and minimize conflicts.

sample_queries.question: Find baggage details by tag number for customer service inquiry
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT b.baggage_id, b.tag_number, b.status AS baggage_status, f.flight_number, f.status AS flight_status,
       bl.loading_time, bu.unloading_time,
       bs.scan_location, bs.scan_time AS last_scan_time
FROM baggage b
JOIN flight f ON b.flight_id = f.flight_id
LEFT JOIN baggage_loading bl ON b.baggage_id = bl.baggage_id
LEFT JOIN baggage_unloading bu ON b.baggage_id = bu.baggage_id
LEFT JOIN (
    SELECT baggage_id, scan_location, scan_time
    FROM baggage_scan
    WHERE scan_time = (SELECT MAX(scan_time) FROM baggage_scan WHERE baggage_id = b.baggage_id)
) bs ON b.baggage_id = bs.baggage_id
WHERE b.tag_number = 'BA123456789';

sample_queries.question: Generate daily turnaround operations report showing all flights with their gate/stand assignments and task completion status
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT to.turnaround_id, f.flight_number, f.status AS flight_status,
       to.start_time, to.target_departure_time, to.status AS turnaround_status,
       ga.gate_number, sa.stand_number,
       bt.gate_ready, bt.baggage_loaded, bt.status AS boarding_status,
       ct.catering_vendor, ct.status AS catering_status,
       clt.status AS cleaning_status,
       rt.fuel_added, rt.status AS refueling_status
FROM turnaround_operation to
JOIN flight f ON to.flight_id = f.flight_id
LEFT JOIN gate_assignment ga ON f.flight_id = ga.flight_id
LEFT JOIN stand_assignment sa ON f.flight_id = sa.flight_id
LEFT JOIN boarding_task bt ON to.turnaround_id = bt.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN catering_task ct ON to.turnaround_id = ct.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN cleaning_task clt ON to.turnaround_id = clt.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN refueling_task rt ON to.turnaround_id = rt.turnaround_id
WHERE to.start_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '1 day'
ORDER BY to.start_time;

sample_queries.question: Calculate average turnaround duration by flight status and identify flights exceeding target departure time
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT f.status AS flight_status,
       COUNT(to.turnaround_id) AS total_turnarounds,
       AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (to.target_departure_time - to.start_time))/60) AS avg_turnaround_minutes,
       SUM(CASE WHEN to.start_time > to.target_departure_time THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS delayed_count,
       ROUND(100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN to.start_time > to.target_departure_time THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(to.turnaround_id), 2) AS delay_percentage
FROM turnaround_operation to
JOIN flight f ON to.flight_id = f.flight_id
WHERE to.start_time >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '30 days'
GROUP BY f.status
ORDER BY avg_turnaround_minutes DESC;

sample_queries.question: Summarize employee count per department with role distribution and attendance rate for the current month
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT d.department_name,
       COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id) AS total_employees,
       STRING_AGG(DISTINCT r.role_name, ', ') AS roles_in_department,
       COUNT(DISTINCT a.employee_id) AS employees_with_attendance,
       ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(DISTINCT a.employee_id) / COUNT(DISTINCT e.employee_id), 2) AS attendance_rate_pct,
       AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (a.check_out - a.check_in))/3600) AS avg_work_hours_per_day
FROM department d
LEFT JOIN employee e ON d.department_id = e.department_id
LEFT JOIN employee_role er ON e.employee_id = er.employee_id
LEFT JOIN role r ON er.role_id = r.role_id
LEFT JOIN attendance a ON e.employee_id = a.employee_id
    AND a.attendance_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE)
    AND a.attendance_date < DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) + INTERVAL '1 month'
GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name
ORDER BY total_employees DESC;

sample_queries.question: Get complete flight operation view linking flight, gate, stand, turnaround, and all associated tasks with assigned employees
sample_queries.sql:
SELECT f.flight_id, f.flight_number, f.status AS flight_status,
       ga.gate_number, ga.assigned_time AS gate_assigned_time,
       sa.stand_number, sa.assigned_time AS stand_assigned_time,
       to.turnaround_id, to.start_time AS turnaround_start, to.target_departure_time, to.status AS turnaround_status,
       bt.boarding_task_id, bt.gate_ready, bt.baggage_loaded, bt.status AS boarding_status,
       ct.catering_task_id, ct.catering_vendor, ct.start_time AS catering_start, ct.end_time AS catering_end, ct.status AS catering_status,
       clt.cleaning_task_id, clt.start_time AS cleaning_start, clt.end_time AS cleaning_end, clt.status AS cleaning_status,
       e.employee_name AS cleaning_employee,
       rt.refueling_task_id, rt.fuel_remaining, rt.fuel_required, rt.fuel_added, rt.status AS refueling_status
FROM flight f
LEFT JOIN gate_assignment ga ON f.flight_id = ga.flight_id
LEFT JOIN gate g ON ga.gate_id = g.gate_id
LEFT JOIN stand_assignment sa ON f.flight_id = sa.flight_id
LEFT JOIN stand s ON sa.stand_id = s.stand_id
LEFT JOIN turnaround_operation to ON f.flight_id = to.flight_id
LEFT JOIN boarding_task bt ON to.turnaround_id = bt.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN catering_task ct ON to.turnaround_id = ct.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN cleaning_task clt ON to.turnaround_id = clt.turnaround_id
LEFT JOIN employee e ON clt.assigned_employee_id = e.employee_id
LEFT JOIN refueling_task rt ON to.turnaround_id = rt.turnaround_id
WHERE f.flight_number = 'AA123'
ORDER BY to.start_time DESC;
